name: US 10-Year Treasury Yield
summary: Day 63 Thursday. US 10Y 4.42% (+1.5% 1D / +7bps; +1.8% 1M; +4.5% 3M) — HIGHEST IN 3M, PRICING STRUCTURAL INFLATION ACCEPTANCE POST-FOMC. The transitory-vs-structural binary RESOLVED to STRUCTURAL on Powell's verbal validation: 'it hasn't even peaked yet.' FOMC HELD 3.5-3.75% with FOUR-WAY DISSENT (most splintered since Oct 1992; 11-1 on rate, 4 dissents on statement collectively — Miran lone dovish for 25bp cut, Hammack/Kashkari/Logan voted-with-majority but OPPOSED easing bias = HAWKISH dissent). The 3:1 hawkish-vs-dovish dissent ratio reinforces the structural read. CME FedWatch: 0% Apr cut, 30% by Dec (down from 70% in Mar). Brent closes $118-120 (+6-8%) on Trump-Axios 'choking like a stuffed pig' rejection of Hormuz-First — Day 1 of $115-sustained trigger CONFIRMED; oil-inflation pass-through tail intensifies. Bond market reaction priced (a) extended-economic-warfare base case → sustained Brent premium → durable energy-inflation, (b) Powell's structural framing on the record, (c) Fed-cut catalyst pushed deeper into 2026. US 2Y 3.59% flat (anchored on Fed-on-hold). India 10Y GOI ~6.97% (Apr 27 read) — Apr 30 IST open faces WORSE setup (Brent +$5 since previous Indian close, DXY firmer, US 10Y +7bps); India 10Y likely tests 7.00% on cascade. Powell confirmed final press conference; Warsh-transition 'very normal, standard.' Goldman regime call empirically vindicated Day 10 — gold underperforming (-0.2% 1D, -13.9% 3M) is cleanest cross-asset confirmation. The 'Fed pivot' narrative is STRUCTURALLY REJECTED. PRIOR DAY-1 SUMMARY (preserved): Brent breached $115 intraday Wed (close $114.64 +3.04%) — 8th straight up session. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]
